# Spec Quality Checklist: Init Command

**Purpose**: Full quality sweep — validate completeness, clarity, consistency, and coverage of all requirements
**Created**: 2026-02-28
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 - Is success output defined — what does the user see after a manifest is successfully created? [Gap]
- [ ] CHK002 - Are exit code requirements specified for success and each failure mode (validation error, file exists, not writable, no TTY)? [Gap]
- [ ] CHK003 - Are stdout vs stderr separation requirements defined for normal output, errors, and prompts? [Gap]
- [ ] CHK004 - Is the prompt order for package fields (name, version, description, license) specified? [Completeness, Spec §FR-002]
- [ ] CHK005 - Are default/suggested values for prompted fields defined (e.g., default version `0.1.0`, default license)? [Gap]
- [ ] CHK006 - Is the behavior for providing type-irrelevant flags specified (e.g., `--type project --version 1.0.0`)? [Gap]
- [ ] CHK007 - Is the behavior for an invalid `--type` flag value specified (e.g., `--type foo`)? [Gap]
- [ ] CHK008 - Are logging requirements specified — what events are logged, at what level, and where? [Gap, referenced in Input but absent from requirements]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK009 - Is SC-001 measurable as written? "Users can create a valid manifest through the interactive flow" has no quantifiable criterion. [Measurability, Spec §SC-001]
- [ ] CHK010 - Is "clear, actionable validation error messages" defined with a format or structure? [Clarity, Spec §FR-013]
- [ ] CHK011 - Is "clear error message" for file-exists guard defined with expected content? [Clarity, Spec §FR-007]
- [ ] CHK012 - Is "hybrid mode" (FR-010) clearly defined — what determines whether to prompt vs error for missing fields? [Clarity, Spec §FR-010]
- [ ] CHK013 - Is the JSON output format specified beyond "2-space indentation and trailing newline" (e.g., field ordering, key quoting)? [Clarity, Assumptions]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK014 - Is the difference between interactive validation failure (re-prompt, US1§2-3) and flag validation failure (exit with error, US3§3) intentional and consistently specified? [Consistency, Spec §US1 vs §US3]
- [ ] CHK015 - Does FR-003 specify that project names follow the same schema validation as package names, consistent with edge case on line 79? [Consistency, Spec §FR-003 vs Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK016 - Are the fields listed in FR-002 and FR-003 consistent with the schema's field availability per type (e.g., is license truly package-only)? [Consistency, Spec §FR-002/003 vs schema]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK017 - Does SC-006 specify where usage examples appear — in `--help` output, man page, or both? [Measurability, Spec §SC-006]
- [ ] CHK018 - Are acceptance scenarios defined for the hybrid mode (partial flags + interactive prompts for the rest)? [Coverage, Spec §FR-010]
- [ ] CHK019 - Are acceptance scenarios defined for `aipkg init --help` output? [Coverage, Spec §FR-014]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK020 - Is a scenario defined for successful non-interactive project creation via flags (`--type project` with no other flags)? [Coverage, Spec §US3]
- [ ] CHK021 - Is a scenario defined for providing `--type` via flag but remaining fields interactively? [Coverage, Spec §FR-010]
- [ ] CHK022 - Is a scenario defined for running `aipkg init` with no flags and no TTY — is this the same as the no-TTY edge case? [Coverage, Edge Cases]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [ ] CHK023 - Is behavior defined for partial file write failure (e.g., disk full mid-write)? Should the partial file be cleaned up? [Edge Case, Gap]
- [ ] CHK024 - Is behavior defined for an existing `aipkg.json` that is a symlink, directory, or unreadable? [Edge Case, Gap]
- [ ] CHK025 - Is behavior defined for conflicting or redundant flags (e.g., `--type project --license MIT` where license is project-irrelevant)? [Edge Case, Gap]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [ ] CHK026 - Is the runtime schema dependency specified — is the JSON schema bundled, fetched, or compiled into the binary? [Dependency, Spec §FR-004]
- [ ] CHK027 - Is the 2-space indentation formatting an assumption or a requirement? If a requirement, should it be in Functional Requirements? [Clarity, Assumptions]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Items marked [Gap] indicate potentially missing requirements
- Items marked with §section references point to existing spec sections to review
- 27 items total across 7 quality dimensions
